AdHocRunResponse.description:
Response shape for ``POST /test-cases/:id/run`` (docs/API.md §3.3).

A thin descriptor the FE uses to deep-link the newly queued run + open the
matching live-events room. ``statusUrl`` is path-relative to the API root so
the client can reuse its existing ``apiClient`` base URL; ``wsRoom`` follows
the canonical ``run:<id>`` channel name shared with the live-events gateway.
AdHocRunResponse.title: AdHocRunResponse

AgentDefinitionBody.description: Request body for POST / PATCH — the raw YAML spec.
AgentDefinitionBody.title: AgentDefinitionBody

AgentDefinitionRead.description: Response schema for a single agent definition.
AgentDefinitionRead.title: AgentDefinitionRead

AgentPluginListResponse.description: Combined list of workspace definitions and system plugins.
AgentPluginListResponse.title: AgentPluginListResponse

AgentPluginSpec.description:
YAML-serialisable descriptor for a custom agent plugin (M8-1).

``name`` must be a valid slug (kebab-case, no spaces) and unique within the
workspace. ``requires_tier`` gates which deployments may activate this plugin.

description.description: Short explanation of what this agent does.
description.title: Description

display_name.description: Human-readable label shown in the UI.
display_name.title: Display Name

model_preference.description: LiteLLM model string (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6'). None falls back to the workspace default.
model_preference.title: Model Preference

name.description: Unique slug (kebab-case). Used as the lookup key in the registry.
name.title: Name

requires_tier.description: Minimum tier required to activate. One of: 'ZERO', 'LOCAL', 'CLOUD'.
requires_tier.title: Requires Tier

system_prompt.description: System prompt injected ahead of the user turn. Max 4000 chars.
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

target_kind_filter.description: TargetKind values this agent handles (e.g. ['BE_REST', 'FE_WEB']). Empty list means the agent handles all target kinds.
target_kind_filter.title: Target Kind Filter

tool_whitelist.description: MCP tool names this agent may call (e.g. 'playwright_mcp.navigate'). Empty list means all tools are permitted.
tool_whitelist.title: Tool Whitelist

AgentPluginSpec.title: AgentPluginSpec

ApiKeyCreateRequest.description: Create a key for the current workspace.
ApiKeyCreateRequest.title: ApiKeyCreateRequest

ApiKeyCreated.description: Returned ONCE on creation — the only time the plaintext key is exposed.
ApiKeyCreated.title: ApiKeyCreated

ApiKeyItem.description:
A key in the management list.

``key`` carries the full plaintext token, decrypted from the AES-GCM column,
so admins can re-copy it. It is ``None`` for keys created before the
encrypted column existed. The list endpoint is admin-gated for this reason.
ApiKeyItem.title: ApiKeyItem

ApiKeyWhoami.description: Verify-probe result: which workspace a key authenticates to.
ApiKeyWhoami.title: ApiKeyWhoami

ArtifactSignedUrl.description:
``GET /runs/:id/artifacts/:artifactId`` — presigned download URL (M1c).

The M1a stub returned a placeholder URL alongside the artifact id + scheme;
M1c replaces it with a real S3 / MinIO presign + the artifact's MIME type
so the FE can decide how to render the response (inline image vs. download).
ArtifactSignedUrl.title: ArtifactSignedUrl

AuditLogRead.description: One audit row in the workspace audit trail (``GET /audit-logs`` item).
AuditLogRead.title: AuditLogRead

AuditLogsResponse.description:
Cursor-paginated envelope for ``GET /audit-logs``.

``next_cursor`` is an opaque base64 token encoding ``(created_at, id)``; when
``None`` the caller has reached the head of the workspace audit log. The
wire key stays snake_case to match the example envelope in ``docs/API.md``
(§179) — the per-row keys are camelCase by alias, only the envelope keys
follow the spec verbatim.
AuditLogsResponse.title: AuditLogsResponse

AuthSection.description:
Auth-related capability flags resolved from process settings (M1e).

``google_oauth_enabled`` is ``True`` only when BOTH the Google OAuth client id
and client secret are configured; the login page renders the Google button
solely off this flag.
AuthSection.title: AuthSection

AutonomyLevel.description: Workspace autonomy dial. ZERO tier is locked to MANUAL.
AutonomyLevel.title: AutonomyLevel

AutonomySection.description: Autonomy levels available + the recommended default for the tier.
AutonomySection.title: AutonomySection

AutonomyUpdate.description: ``PUT`` body — the new level + overrides (+ optional audit reason).
AutonomyUpdate.title: AutonomyUpdate

ChatMessageInput.description: One prior turn in the conversation history.
ChatMessageInput.title: ChatMessageInput

ChatRequest.description: ``POST /agent/chat`` body — the running history + optional session id.
ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest

ConnectionTestResponse.description:
JSON response for ``/test`` + pre-save ``test-connection`` endpoints.

Mirrors :class:`suitest_api.integrations.base.ConnectionTestResult` 1:1 so
the FE can render the same UI for both pre-save and post-save flows. On
success ``ok=True`` and ``account_id``/``display_name`` describe who the
adapter authenticated as. On failure ``ok=False`` and ``error`` carries
the human-readable string the FE renders inline.
ConnectionTestResponse.title: ConnectionTestResponse

CrawlerAuthConfig.description:
Optional pre-crawl authentication. ``kind="none"`` (default) skips auth.

The crawler does not itself perform login today — the config is captured on
the :class:`~suitest_db.models.generator_run.GeneratorRun` for provenance and
threaded into generated cases so a later run can replay the auth context.
CrawlerAuthConfig.title: CrawlerAuthConfig

CrawlerGenerateRequest.description: Crawl request: a target suite + a start URL + bounds/auth.
CrawlerGenerateRequest.title: CrawlerGenerateRequest

CrawlerOptions.title: CrawlerOptions

CreateRunBody.description: ``POST /runs`` body. Mixed snake / camel aliases for FE convenience.
CreateRunBody.title: CreateRunBody

CreateSuiteRunBody.description:
``POST /suites/{id}/run`` body — run every active case in a suite as a bundle.

All fields optional: the selection is derived server-side from the suite's
active cases (in suite order), so the caller only supplies run metadata. ``name``
defaults to the suite name when omitted. This is the QA "run smoke/regression
suite" entry point — one Run row fanning out over N cases.
CreateSuiteRunBody.title: CreateSuiteRunBody

DefectDetail.description: Detail — adds linked resource public ids + external issues.
DefectDetail.title: DefectDetail

DefectTimelineEntry.description: One ordered event in a defect's history (creation + each audit row).
DefectTimelineEntry.title: DefectTimelineEntry

DefectUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /defects/:id`` — status / severity / assignee / description.

``force`` (default ``False``) lets QA+ override the linear status flow for
workflow corrections (e.g. CLOSED → OPEN reopen after a real regression).
Without ``force`` a non-allowed transition returns 400
``INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION``.
DefectUpdate.title: DefectUpdate

DocumentDetail.description: Detail for ``GET /documents/:id`` — no chunk bodies in M1a.
DocumentDetail.title: DocumentDetail

DocumentListItem.description: List row for ``GET /documents`` with a computed ``chunk_count``.
DocumentListItem.title: DocumentListItem

EmbeddingsSection.description: Embeddings backend info (independent of LLM tier).
EmbeddingsSection.title: EmbeddingsSection

EvalFixtureResult.description: Per-fixture pass/fail row in the report.
EvalFixtureResult.title: EvalFixtureResult

EvalFixturesEnvelope.description: ``GET /eval/fixtures`` — the bundled golden datasets the CI run scores.
EvalFixturesEnvelope.title: EvalFixturesEnvelope

EvalRunListItem.description: One row in the score-regression dashboard (M5-2).
EvalRunListItem.title: EvalRunListItem

EvalSuiteInfo.description: One golden dataset suite + its fixture count (M5-2).
EvalSuiteInfo.title: EvalSuiteInfo

FeaturesSection.description: The 13 capability feature flags resolved from (tier, embeddings).
FeaturesSection.title: FeaturesSection

FileSignedUrl.description: Time-limited presigned GET URL for an owned object.
FileSignedUrl.title: FileSignedUrl

FileUploadResult.description: Result of storing an uploaded object server-side.
FileUploadResult.title: FileUploadResult

GitHubTestConnectionRequest.description:
Pre-save credential payload for ``POST /integrations/github/test-connection``.

No row is persisted. ``private_key_pem`` is never logged and never stored
by this endpoint (per ``docs/API.md §3.9``). The router spawns an ephemeral
``github-mcp-server`` process, derives an installation token, calls a
cheap whoami / list-repos tool, and discards.
GitHubTestConnectionRequest.title: GitHubTestConnectionRequest

HeatmapCell.description: One run-count cell in the day x hour grid.
HeatmapCell.title: HeatmapCell

InboxItem.description: One notification card in the Inbox feed.
InboxItem.title: InboxItem

InboxResponse.description: ``GET /inbox`` envelope — items list + unread badge counter.
InboxResponse.title: InboxResponse

IntegrationCreate.description:
``POST /integrations`` request body.

``secrets`` is an arbitrary JSON-object the kind-specific adapter consumes
(e.g. Slack: ``{"webhook_url": "..."}``, Jira: ``{"jira_token": "..."}``).
The service AES-GCM-encrypts it via ``packages/core/crypto`` before INSERT.
``status`` defaults to ``active`` and is set by the service — accepting it
from the wire would let an admin force-set bypassed states.

secrets.description: Adapter-specific secret material (e.g. Slack webhook_url, Jira token). Encrypted at rest via AES-GCM; never echoed back.
secrets.title: Secrets

IntegrationCreate.title: IntegrationCreate

IntegrationDetail.description: Detail — adds visible ``config`` and the redacted ``secrets`` block.
IntegrationDetail.title: IntegrationDetail

IntegrationListItem.description: List row for ``GET /integrations`` (config visible, no secret material).
IntegrationListItem.title: IntegrationListItem

IntegrationRead.description:
Write-path response: ``config`` is visible but secrets stay opaque.

Deliberately ``model_config`` does NOT add a ``secrets`` field — the only
cue the FE gets that secrets exist is ``has_secrets: bool``. The ``config``
field is allowed because integration config (``Jira url``, ``Slack channel
name``) is non-sensitive metadata; the AES-GCM blob lives only in the
database column. See ``docs/API.md §3.9``.
IntegrationRead.title: IntegrationRead

IntegrationUpdate.description:
``PATCH /integrations/:id`` request body — partial update.

``secrets`` semantics:

* absent from body → existing encrypted blob preserved verbatim.
* present but empty dict → existing blob CLEARED (the integration becomes
  secret-less; ``has_secrets`` flips to False).
* present with keys → MERGED with the existing decrypted dict before
  re-encrypting (FE doesn't have to know every secret key). Submitted keys
  overwrite; unsubmitted keys retain prior values.
IntegrationUpdate.title: IntegrationUpdate

JiraSyncedResponse.description: 202 response when the Jira webhook successfully syncs a defect status.
JiraSyncedResponse.title: JiraSyncedResponse

JiraTestConnectionRequest.description:
Pre-save credential payload for ``POST /integrations/jira/test-connection``.

No row is persisted; the router spawns an ephemeral ``jirac-mcp`` adapter
with these creds, calls ``test_connection`` (``GET /rest/api/3/myself``),
and discards the process. ``jira_auth_type`` selects between cloud OAuth
token (``cloud_token``) and PAT (``pat``). M1d ships PAT-only — OAuth flow
deferred to v1.x.
JiraTestConnectionRequest.title: JiraTestConnectionRequest

KpisOut.description: ``GET /analytics/kpis`` → pass rate, run count, avg duration, open defects.
KpisOut.title: KpisOut

LLMConfigPublic.description: Active config, key redacted to a hint.
LLMConfigPublic.title: LLMConfigPublic

LLMSection.description: LLM provider info. ``provider`` is ``"none"`` in ZERO tier.
LLMSection.title: LLMSection

LlmCompleteRequest.description:
One-shot completion request. Kept deliberately small — this is a proxy
for lifecycle codegen/enrichment, not a chat surface (that's /agent/chat).
LlmCompleteRequest.title: LlmCompleteRequest

MatrixRequirement.description: One requirement row: its public id, title, and linked test/defect public ids.
MatrixRequirement.title: MatrixRequirement

McpDiscoveryGenerateRequest.description:
LLM-driven MCP tool-discovery generation (M3-9) — CLOUD/LOCAL only.

Targets a registered MCP provider by id; the agent explores its persisted
tool catalog and proposes contract cases (happy + negative per tool). Steps
are agentic (code translated at execution time, M3-10).
McpDiscoveryGenerateRequest.title: McpDiscoveryGenerateRequest

McpInvokeResult.description: Normalized result of an ad-hoc tool invocation (tool browser).
McpInvokeResult.title: McpInvokeResult

McpProviderCreateBody.description: ``POST /mcp/providers`` body — register a custom MCP server.
McpProviderCreateBody.title: McpProviderCreateBody

McpProviderDetail.description: Detail row — summary + config preview (secrets redacted) + version pins.
McpProviderDetail.title: McpProviderDetail

McpProviderTool.description: One tool entry (name + description + flattened arg schema preview).
McpProviderTool.title: McpProviderTool

McpProviderUpdateBody.description: ``PATCH /mcp/providers/:id`` body — partial update of a custom row.
McpProviderUpdateBody.title: McpProviderUpdateBody

McpRoutingResponse.description: ``GET /mcp/routing`` — default table overlaid with workspace overrides.
McpRoutingResponse.title: McpRoutingResponse

McpRoutingUpdateBody.description: ``PUT /mcp/routing`` body — replace the workspace override map.
McpRoutingUpdateBody.title: McpRoutingUpdateBody

MeResponse.description: ``GET /auth/me`` — the current user plus every workspace they belong to.
MeResponse.title: MeResponse

MembershipPublic.description: A user's membership in a workspace, with the workspace embedded.
MembershipPublic.title: MembershipPublic

NetworkEvent.description: One network event captured during a run (HAR-derived, M1c).
NetworkEvent.title: NetworkEvent

OpenApiGeneratorOptions.description:
Per-request toggles for which case kinds the generator emits.

Defaults emit the full deterministic suite; callers disable categories to
keep a generated suite focused (e.g. contract-only). ``tags_filter`` limits
generation to operations carrying at least one of the listed OpenAPI tags.
OpenApiGeneratorOptions.title: OpenApiGeneratorOptions

PageMeta.description: Pagination metadata: the next cursor (``null`` when exhausted) + page size.
PageMeta.title: PageMeta

PassRateSeriesOut.description: ``GET /analytics/pass-rate`` → ascending time series + total sample size.
PassRateSeriesOut.title: PassRateSeriesOut

PrdGenerateRequest.description:
LLM-driven PRD generation (M3-6) — CLOUD/LOCAL only.

``prd_text`` is the requirement / user story / free text. The agent extracts
stories and drafts happy-path + edge cases. ``default_target_kind`` decides
the steps' default ``mcp_provider`` (steps are agentic — code is translated at
execution time, M3-10). ``seed`` is threaded for reproducibility (M3-5).
PrdGenerateRequest.title: PrdGenerateRequest

ProjectCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /projects`` (docs/API.md §3.2).

``slug`` is optional — when omitted the service derives it from ``name``
via :func:`suitest_api.utils.slug.slugify` and retries with a ``-2``
suffix once on collision before bubbling 409.
ProjectCreate.title: ProjectCreate

ProjectUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /projects/:id`` (docs/API.md §3.2).

Slug is **immutable** post-create — attempting to PATCH it raises a 400
``IMMUTABLE_SLUG``. ``gating_suite_id`` must reference a suite that lives
in this project (cross-project assignments raise 400
``INVALID_GATING_SUITE``). All fields optional; only
``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`` keys are applied.
ProjectUpdate.title: ProjectUpdate

PromptDefaultPublic.description: A file-based default prompt + whether the workspace forks it.
PromptDefaultPublic.title: PromptDefaultPublic

PromptDetailPublic.description: ``GET /prompts/:name`` — default content + the workspace's fork history.
PromptDetailPublic.title: PromptDetailPublic

PromptExperimentPublic.description: One prompt A/B experiment + live stats.
PromptExperimentPublic.title: PromptExperimentPublic

PromptForkPublic.description: One versioned fork of a prompt for a workspace.
PromptForkPublic.title: PromptForkPublic

PromptListEnvelope.description: ``GET /prompts`` — every overridable default + its fork status.
PromptListEnvelope.title: PromptListEnvelope

RecorderFinalizeRequest.description: Body for ``POST .../finalize`` — where + how to persist the new case.
RecorderFinalizeRequest.title: RecorderFinalizeRequest

RecorderSessionStartResponse.description: 200 body — the session id + the WS room to subscribe for live events.
RecorderSessionStartResponse.title: RecorderSessionStartResponse

RequirementCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /requirements`` (docs/API.md §3.7).

``project_id`` is workspace-scoped (cross-workspace ids return 404). The
``REQ-N`` ``public_id`` is assigned by the ``before_insert`` listener via
``generate_public_id`` (docs/DATA_MODEL §8) — never accepted from the caller.
RequirementCreate.title: RequirementCreate

RequirementDetail.description: Detail with the public ids of linked cases + linked defects.
RequirementDetail.title: RequirementDetail

RequirementLinkCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /requirements/:id/links`` — ``{ "testCaseId": "..." }``.

Accepts both ``test_case_id`` (Pythonic) and ``testCaseId`` / ``caseId``
(docs/API.md §3.7 wire shape) for FE compatibility.
RequirementLinkCreate.title: RequirementLinkCreate

RequirementListItem.description: List row with a computed ``link_count`` (number of linked cases).
RequirementListItem.title: RequirementListItem

RequirementUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /requirements/:id`` — metadata patch.

All fields optional; only ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`` keys are
applied. Sending ``null`` explicitly clears the field (description / source
/ external_url are nullable in the model).
RequirementUpdate.title: RequirementUpdate

RunDetail.description: Detail for ``GET /runs/:id`` — adds the computed summary.
RunDetail.title: RunDetail

RunLogItem.description:
One persisted ``run_step_logs`` row in the page (M1c).

``message`` is the JSON-encoded event payload the orchestrator published —
the FE deserialises it on receipt to match the live socket stream.
RunLogItem.title: RunLogItem

RunLogPage.description: A cursor-paginated slice of a run's persisted log stream (M1c).
RunLogPage.title: RunLogPage

RunPublic.description:
``POST /runs`` + ``cancel`` + ``rerun`` response shape.

Sized for the runs list/detail FE rows so cancel/rerun results render in
place without a follow-up GET. Aliased to camelCase on serialization to
match the M1b client.
RunPublic.title: RunPublic

RunReplayStep.description: One step in the time-travel replay, with its state delta vs. the prior step.
RunReplayStep.title: RunReplayStep

RunSelectionItem.description:
One ``{caseId, selectedStepIds?}`` entry in the create-run selection.

``selected_step_ids`` is optional — when ``None`` the runner picks every
active step on the case. M1c keeps this opt-in flag in the request envelope
so a future "rerun only failed steps" UI can wire through unchanged.
RunSelectionItem.title: RunSelectionItem

RunStepPublic.description: One run step with its outcome + linked case public id (docs/API.md §3.5).
RunStepPublic.title: RunStepPublic

RunSummary.description: Aggregate step outcomes for a run.
RunSummary.title: RunSummary

RunsSummary.description:
``GET /runs/summary`` — counters for the Runs dashboard summary bar.

Field aliases are camelCase to match the M1b frontend client.
``failed`` folds ``FAIL`` + ``ERROR``; ``avg_duration_ms`` is a workspace-wide
weighted mean across non-null durations.
RunsSummary.title: RunsSummary

SecretsHint.description: Redacted secret marker + last-4 hint (no full secret ever).
SecretsHint.title: SecretsHint

StateChangePublic.description: One key-level state change at a replay step (M5-1).
StateChangePublic.title: StateChangePublic

StepCreate.description:
One step inside a :class:`TestCaseCreate` / :class:`StepReplace` payload.

``order`` is honoured if provided; otherwise the service assigns sequential
1-based positions in array order. ``code`` MAY be omitted in CLOUD / LOCAL
tiers (or when ``workspace.strict_zero_validation=false``); ZERO tier with
strict validation rejects via ``STEPS_REQUIRE_CODE_IN_ZERO_LLM``.
StepCreate.title: StepCreate

SuiteCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /suites``.

``order`` defaults to ``0``; callers usually leave it blank and let the
repository's ``order ASC, created_at DESC`` ordering position the suite.
SuiteCreate.title: SuiteCreate

SuitePublic.description: A suite with its non-deleted ``case_count`` (set by the service).
SuitePublic.title: SuitePublic

SuiteUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /suites/:id`` (docs/API.md §3.4).

All fields optional; only ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`` keys are
applied. ``case_order`` (when present) drives an atomic reorder of every
active case in the suite — the submitted id set must match the live set
exactly or a 400 with ``details.missing`` / ``details.unknown`` lands.
SuiteUpdate.title: SuiteUpdate

SyncConflict.description:
One entry in :class:`SyncResult.conflicts` — local state diverges from remote.

Surfaces the defect public id + the local / remote DefectStatus strings so
the FE renders a clear "external system says X but Suitest says Y" prompt.
SyncConflict.title: SyncConflict

SyncResult.description:
``POST /integrations/:id/sync`` response.

``synced`` counts defects whose ``status`` was updated to match the remote
system. ``conflicts`` lists defects whose remote state could not be applied
without overwriting a manual change (e.g. local CLOSED, remote OPEN → the
sync respects manual override). ``skipped`` counts defects ignored because
they were already in a terminal status.
SyncResult.title: SyncResult

SystemPluginRead.description: Response schema for a system (entry-point-discovered) plugin.
SystemPluginRead.title: SystemPluginRead

TestCaseListItem.description: List row — metadata only, no steps (docs/API.md §3.3).
TestCaseListItem.title: TestCaseListItem

TestCaseUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /test-cases/:id`` — metadata + tag replace.

All fields optional; only ``model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`` keys are
applied. Tags, if provided, replace the existing set in full.
TestCaseUpdate.title: TestCaseUpdate

Tier.description:
Capability tier. The env base is always ZERO; LOCAL/CLOUD come from the
per-workspace LLM config (web UI).
Tier.title: Tier

UrlSemanticGenerateRequest.description:
LLM-driven semantic URL generation (M3-7) — CLOUD/LOCAL only.

Decomposes a natural-language ``intent`` ("checkout flow") into FE_WEB
journey cases on ``url``. Steps are agentic browser actions driven by
``playwright-mcp`` (code translated at execution time, M3-10).
UrlSemanticGenerateRequest.title: UrlSemanticGenerateRequest

WebhookEnqueuedResponse.description: 202 response when the webhook successfully enqueues a gating run.
WebhookEnqueuedResponse.title: WebhookEnqueuedResponse

WebhookIgnoredResponse.description:
200 response when the receiver intentionally drops the event.

``reason`` is a stable machine-readable string so dashboards and CI plugins
can branch on it without parsing free-form messages. Known reasons:

* ``duplicate`` — Redis SETNX dedup hit within the TTL window.
* ``no_gating_suite`` — neither ``gating_suite_id`` nor any ``smoke``-tagged
  cases are configured for the project (Q4 default).
* ``unsupported_event`` — webhook event kind isn't one the receiver acts on.
* ``unsupported_action`` — Merge Request / PR action that does not enqueue a run.
* ``branch_deleted`` — GitHub push with ``deleted=true``.
* ``unknown_issue`` — no local defect carries the inbound issue key.
* ``unmappable_status`` — adapter's status map returned ``None`` for the
  external status name.
* ``no_status_change`` — mapped status already matches the local defect's
  status (idempotent replay).
WebhookIgnoredResponse.title: WebhookIgnoredResponse

WebhookPingResponse.description:
200 response to a GitHub ``ping`` event.

GitHub fires ``ping`` when a hook is first registered; returning
``{pong: true}`` matches GitHub's documented expectation and unblocks the
hook-config UI's "Recent Deliveries" smoke check.
WebhookPingResponse.title: WebhookPingResponse

WorkspaceCreate.description:
``POST /workspaces`` body — bootstrap a workspace; caller becomes OWNER.

``slug`` is optional: when omitted it is derived from ``name`` (kebab-case,
one ``-2`` retry on collision). Workspace slugs are globally unique, so a
second collision surfaces a 409 ``DUPLICATE_WORKSPACE_SLUG``.
WorkspaceCreate.title: WorkspaceCreate

WorkspaceDetail.description: ``GET /workspaces/:id`` — Settings General tab consumes the extra fields.
WorkspaceDetail.title: WorkspaceDetail

WorkspaceExportStatus.description: ``GET /workspaces/:id/export/:job_id`` — poll result, surfaces the signed URL.
WorkspaceExportStatus.title: WorkspaceExportStatus

WorkspacePublic.description: Workspace summary used in lists + as the nested object in a membership.
WorkspacePublic.title: WorkspacePublic

_BulkDeletePayload.description: ``delete`` action — empty payload. Accept `{}` and absent keys both.
_BulkDeletePayload.title: _BulkDeletePayload

_BulkMoveToSuitePayload.description: ``move_to_suite`` action — target suite id (must be same workspace).
_BulkMoveToSuitePayload.title: _BulkMoveToSuitePayload

_BulkSetPriorityPayload.description: ``set_priority`` action — wire format uses the :class:`Priority` enum.
_BulkSetPriorityPayload.title: _BulkSetPriorityPayload

suitest_api__routers__mcp_providers__Mcp.description: Summary row — name + transport + health + tool names (no secrets).
suitest_api__routers__mcp_providers__Mcp.title: McpProviderPublic

suitest_shared__schemas__capabilities__M.description: Public MCP provider entry — same shape as ``GET /mcp/providers`` rows.
suitest_shared__schemas__capabilities__M.title: McpProviderPublic

get.description: List workspace agent definitions and system-discovered plugins.

post.description: Register a new workspace agent definition from a YAML spec.

get.description: Return a single workspace agent definition by name.

patch.description: Update the YAML spec for an existing workspace agent definition.

post.description: Stream a conversation-mode reply (SSE tokens + WS tool events).

get.description: Coverage by suite (cases with a requirement link) + by requirement.
Flaky cases above ``minRate`` (reuses the Task 4 AnalyticsService rule).

schema.title: Response Analytics Flaky Api V1 Analytics Flaky Get

get.description: Run-count grid (day x hour) over the window (docs/API.md §3.8).

schema.title: Response Analytics Heatmap Api V1 Analytics Heatmap Get

get.description: Pass rate, run count, avg duration, and open defect count for the window.
Daily pass-rate time series (ascending by date) over the window.
Deterministic release-readiness score + blocker list (no LLM).

Verify a key: returns the workspace it authenticates to. 401 if invalid.

Used by the CLI / MCP setup to confirm ``SUITEST_API_KEY`` works before use.
List workspace audit rows newest-first with cursor pagination + filters.

parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior page.
parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque cursor from a prior page.
schema.title: Cursor

post.description:
Enqueue re-import of an archived audit month (M4-32) for this workspace.

The month lives in MinIO cold storage as
``s3://<archive>/audit/<workspace>/<YYYY-MM>.jsonl.gz``; the ``restore_audit_logs``
runner job pulls it back into the hot table (idempotent on row id). ADMIN+.

schema.title: Response Restore Audit Month Api V1 Audit Restore Post

get.description: Return the authenticated user plus every workspace membership they hold.
List the workspace's defects with filters, keyset-paginated.

post.description: Manually file a defect (``SUIT-N`` public id, ``created_by='user:<id>'``).

get.description: Return a defect with linked public ids + external issues; 404 if cross-ws.

patch.description: Patch status / severity / assignee / description; honours the transition matrix.

post.description:
Force re-sync the defect to the configured external tracker.

Returns 404 for cross-workspace ids before raising 501
``ADAPTER_NOT_REGISTERED`` — the adapter registry lands in M1d-11+ and
will replace the unconditional 501 with real Jira / Linear dispatch.

get.description: Return the defect's creation event + audit rows in ascending time; 404 if cross-ws.

schema.title: Response Get Defect Timeline Api V1 Defects  Defect Id  Timeline Get

get.description: List the workspace's indexed documents (optionally by kind), paginated.
Return one document with its chunk_count; 404 when cross-workspace.
List the bundled golden datasets + fixture counts the weekly CI scores (M5-2).
Newest-first eval run history for the score-regression dashboard (M5-2).

post.description: Run the deterministic eval suite over bundled fixtures + persist the result.

get.description: Fetch one eval run; 404 if missing or owned by another workspace.

delete.description: Delete an object the caller owns (no-op-safe; 404 for out-of-scope keys).

post.description: Store an uploaded blob in the workspace's object store; return its s3:// URL.

get.description: Return a presigned GET URL for an object the caller owns (404 otherwise).

parameters.description: Workspace-scoped object key returned by the upload.
parameters.name: key

schema.description: Workspace-scoped object key returned by the upload.
schema.title: Key

post.description: Classify a generation input into a target kind + recommended strategy/MCP.
Generate a smoke + form suite by crawling a start URL (SSE).

Select relevant test cases for a PR diff via LLM impact analysis (M6).

At CLOUD/LOCAL tier the endpoint queries the active LLM to identify which
cases are most likely to catch regressions introduced by ``diff_text``.
At ZERO tier (or when no LLM is configured) it returns **all** cases in
the suite so CI always has a safe fallback.

``diff_text`` must not exceed 50 000 characters; a 400 is returned if it
does.  The suite must belong to the caller's workspace; a 404 is returned
when the suite has no cases.
Generate DRAFT cases by exploring a registered MCP provider's tools (SSE).
Generate a per-operation contract suite from an OpenAPI 3.0 spec (SSE).
Generate DRAFT cases from a PRD / user story via the LLM agent (SSE).
Open a live browser-recording session. Returns the WS room to subscribe.

delete.description: Cancel an active recording session (idempotent within its lifetime).

post.description: Convert a session's captured events into a DRAFT TestCase + return it.
Generate FE_WEB journey cases from a URL + natural-language intent (SSE).

get.description: List inbox items for the active workspace (empty stub in M1a).

post.description: Validate/repair a publisher's project binding (read-only, never creates).

get.description: List the workspace's integrations (optionally filtered by kind).

schema.title: Response List Integrations Api V1 Integrations Get

post.description: Connect a new integration. ADMIN/OWNER; secrets AES-GCM encrypted; never echoed.
Validate GitHub App credentials BEFORE persisting. Never logs ``private_key_pem``.
Validate Jira credentials BEFORE persisting. Never logs ``jira_token``.

delete.description: Hard-delete an integration. ADMIN/OWNER. No body.

get.description: Return an integration with config + REDACTED secrets; 404 if cross-workspace.

patch.description: Patch an integration. Partial; absent ``secrets`` preserves the existing blob.

post.description: Refetch external statuses for every linked defect; update local status with conflict reporting.
Smoke-test an existing integration's credentials. Never 500s on bad creds.
Proxy one completion through the workspace's active LLM provider.

get.description: List MCP providers — bundled builtins (pinned) + custom workspace rows.

post.description:
Register a custom MCP server.

With ``validate=true`` (default) the server connects, performs the MCP
``initialize`` handshake, runs ``tools/list``, and persists the discovered
catalog + ``health_status=ok`` + version pins (M2-7). A failed probe rejects
the registration with ``422 MCP_REGISTRATION_FAILED`` and writes no row.

Dry-run connect + ``tools/list`` without persisting (M2-7 register modal).

Lets the UI flip the form's status pill before the user saves. Failures
surface as ``422 MCP_REGISTRATION_FAILED``.

delete.description: Delete a custom provider. Builtins are read-only (409).

get.description: Provider detail — config preview + discovered tool catalog (no secrets).

patch.description: Patch a custom provider. Builtins are read-only (409).

post.description:
Re-run ``tools/list`` against a custom provider and persist the catalog (M2-8).

Updates ``config_json.tools`` + ``health_status`` + ``last_health_at`` (+
``version_pin`` when the server advertises one). Builtins are read-only (409).

Dev-aid: invoke one tool ad-hoc against a custom provider (tool browser).

Role-gated to ``ADMIN``+ (MCP_PLUGINS §11). Every call is audit-logged with
``invocation_source=tool_browser`` and an ``arg_hash`` (raw args are not
persisted). Builtins are not ad-hoc invokable here (409) — they run through
the runner. Tool failures surface as ``ok=false`` with the error message.

get.description: Effective routing table — bundled defaults overlaid with workspace overrides.

put.description:
Replace the workspace routing overrides (consumed by the runner).

Each key must be a valid ``target_kind`` and every referenced provider
(``primary`` + ``fallback``) must be a known, enabled provider in the
workspace, else ``422``. Stored under
``workspace_capabilities.features_json.routing_overrides`` in the
``{primary, fallback}`` shape :func:`suitest_mcp.routing.resolve_provider`
consumes.

get.description: Return the kinds of all registered custom integration adapters.

schema.title: Response List Integration Adapters Api V1 Plugins Integration Adapters Get

get.description: Return all registered plugin manifests, optionally filtered by type.

schema.title: Response List Marketplace Api V1 Plugins Marketplace Get

post.description:
Register a new community plugin manifest.

Only ADMIN or OWNER can submit.  Name must be globally unique.

get.description: Return a single plugin manifest by its unique name.
get.summary: Get one plugin manifest by name (public)

get.description: Return custom MCP providers discovered via the suitest.mcp_providers entry group.

schema.title: Response List Custom Mcp Providers Api V1 Plugins Mcp Providers Get

get.summary: List discovered custom MCP entry-point providers

get.description: Return the names of all registered reporter plugin instances.

schema.title: Response List Reporters Api V1 Plugins Reporters Get

get.description: List the current workspace's active projects, keyset-paginated.

post.description: Create a project under the active workspace (ADMIN/OWNER only).

delete.description:
Soft-delete the project + cascade child suites + cases (with ``confirmCascade=true``).

Returns 204 on success. Without ``confirmCascade=true`` against a project
that has at least one active child suite, returns 409
``CONFIRM_CASCADE_REQUIRED`` with ``suiteCount`` / ``caseCount`` /
``resourceType``.

get.description: Return one active project; 404 when cross-workspace or tombstoned.

patch.description:
Patch metadata (ADMIN/OWNER only).

Slug is immutable — PATCH with a ``slug`` field returns 400
``IMMUTABLE_SLUG``. ``gating_suite_id`` must belong to the target project,
else 400 ``INVALID_GATING_SUITE``.

post.description:
Revive a soft-deleted project (idempotent; children stay tombstoned).

Re-POST after a successful restore returns 204 (idempotent). Does NOT
auto-restore child suites + cases — restore each individually via
``POST /suites/:id/restore`` / ``POST /test-cases/:id/restore``.

get.description: List the workspace's prompt A/B experiments with live stats.

post.description:
Start an A/B test between two variants of ``prompt_name``.

A variant override id of ``null`` means the file default. The prompt must
exist as a file default; referenced forks must belong to this workspace.
Record a success/failure outcome for a variant (any workspace member).
Stop an experiment (subsequent resolution falls back to fork/default).

get.description: List every overridable default prompt + whether the workspace forks it.

delete.description: Delete a fork. Reverts the prompt to the file default if it was active.

post.description: Make a specific fork the active override for its prompt.

get.description: Return a prompt's file default content + the workspace's fork history.

post.description: Create a new versioned fork of ``prompt_name`` for the workspace.

get.description: List a project's requirements with link counts; 404 when cross-workspace.

post.description: Create a requirement under ``project_id``; ``REQ-N`` public id is assigned by listener.

delete.description: Soft-delete; subsequent re-delete returns 404 (the row is already hidden).

get.description:
Return a requirement with linked case + defect public ids; 404 if cross-ws.

Soft-deleted requirements are invisible (404) to keep the public id namespace
consistent with the LIST endpoint. Use ``POST /restore`` to bring them back.

patch.description: Patch metadata; only present fields are applied (``exclude_unset=True``).

post.description:
Link a requirement to a test case. Same workspace required.

Cross-workspace ``test_case_id`` → 400 ``CROSS_WORKSPACE_LINK`` (both ws ids
in ``details``). Idempotent: re-POSTing an existing pair returns ``201`` with
the existing link id (no extra audit / WS row).

schema.title: Response Create Requirement Link Api V1 Requirements  Requirement Id  Links Post

delete.description: Remove a link. 404 when the requirement is out-of-scope OR the link is gone.

post.description: Clear the ``deleted_at`` tombstone. Idempotent on already-active rows.

get.description: List a project's runs with filters; 404 when the project is cross-workspace.

post.description:
Validate selection + MCP routing, persist the run, enqueue the ARQ job.

Returns 202 once the row exists and the job has been enqueued — the runner
flips the status to ``RUNNING`` / ``PASS`` / ``FAIL`` asynchronously. The
metadata blob carries the original selection so a rerun (or the orchestrator
on resume) can rehydrate it without re-deriving suite ordering.
Record an already-completed run (run + run_steps + artifacts). No ARQ enqueue.

get.description:
Aggregated counters for the Runs dashboard summary bar (docs/API.md §3.5).

Counts are workspace-scoped (joined via ``projects``). ``failed`` folds
``FAIL`` + ``ERROR`` together to match the Runs UI's binary outcome card.
Static endpoint declared BEFORE the dynamic ``/runs/{run_id}`` route below
so FastAPI's path matcher doesn't try to treat ``summary`` as a run id.

Return a run with a step-outcome summary; 404 when cross-workspace.

Accepts the internal id OR the per-workspace ``public_id`` (e.g. ``R-1004``)
that the web routes on.
List a run's artifacts; 404 when the run is cross-workspace.

schema.title: Response Get Run Artifacts Api V1 Runs  Run Id  Artifacts Get

get.description:
Return a real S3/MinIO presigned download URL for one artifact (M1c Task 18).

Replaces the M1a stub presigner with an :mod:`aioboto3` ``generate_presigned_url``
call against the configured bucket. Only ``s3://...`` artifacts are presigned —
legacy ``file://`` artifacts (dev fixtures) return 404 here, the client should
fall back to the static ``/artifacts/raw/`` route the static server exposes.
Emits an ``artifact.signed_url`` audit row so download attribution is
captured even though the actual fetch happens directly against S3.

post.description:
Transition a QUEUED / RUNNING run to CANCELLED and best-effort abort the ARQ job.

The DB transition is authoritative — even if ARQ is unreachable, the run
row flips to CANCELLED so the UI no longer shows it as live. We then try
to abort the in-flight job (no-op if the job already completed / never
started). Returns 409 when the run is already in a terminal state.

get.description:
Cursor-paginated slice of the orchestrator's persisted log stream (M1c).

``cursor`` is the last ``seq`` the client has already seen — pass ``0`` to
fetch the head of the stream. Returns up to ``limit`` rows ordered ascending
by ``seq`` plus the next ``seq`` for follow-up paging and a boolean
``hasMore`` so the FE knows when to stop polling. A request that returns
fewer rows than ``limit`` is the natural EOF marker.

Network events captured during the run (M1b stub).

Validates run-in-workspace scope (404 cross-workspace) and returns an empty
page — HAR-driven event extraction lands with the runner in M1c. Frontend
Network tab already renders the empty state today, so wiring this stub now
means the screen stops 404-ing in real dev.

Time-travel replay: ordered steps + per-step state delta (M5-1).

Deterministic / ZERO-tier — the delta is a pure JSON diff between each step's
captured ``state_snapshot`` (normalized MCP output) and the previous step's.
The first step has an empty delta (no prior state). 404 when cross-workspace.

Render a run as a JUnit XML report for CI consumption (Jenkins / GHA).

Deterministic / ZERO-tier: each test case becomes one ``<testcase>`` rolled up
from its run steps (error > failure > skipped > passed). 404 when cross-workspace.
Returned as ``application/xml`` so a CI job can pipe it into its test reporter.

post.description:
Clone the source run's selection into a fresh QUEUED row + enqueue the ARQ job.

A rerun reuses the original selection + routing override (so the runner
fans out identically) but re-resolves the workspace tier so a tier change
between the two runs is honored. Returns 202 like the create endpoint.

get.description: Return a run's steps (ordered) with outcomes + case public ids; 404 if cross-ws.

schema.title: Response Get Run Steps Api V1 Runs  Run Id  Steps Get

get.description: List active (non-deleted) suites for a project; 404 cross-workspace.

schema.title: Response List Suites Api V1 Suites Get

post.description: Create a suite under an in-scope project (QA+ only).

delete.description:
Soft-delete the suite + cascade child cases (with ``confirmCascade=true``).

Returns 204 on success. Without ``confirmCascade=true`` against a suite
that has at least one active child case, returns 409
``CONFIRM_CASCADE_REQUIRED`` with ``childCount`` + ``resourceType``.

get.description: Return one suite with its case_count; 404 when cross-workspace.

patch.description: Patch metadata + optional atomic ``case_order`` reorder (QA+ only).

post.description:
Revive a soft-deleted suite (idempotent; children stay tombstoned).

Re-POST after a successful restore returns the same 204 (per
docs/API.md §328). Does not auto-restore child cases — restore each
individually via ``POST /test-cases/:id/restore``.

Run every active case in a suite as ONE bundle run, then enqueue the ARQ job.

QA "run smoke/regression suite" entry point: the selection is derived
server-side from the suite's active cases (in suite order), so the caller only
sends run metadata. Returns 202 like ``POST /runs``. 400 when the suite is
cross-workspace ("suite not found") or empty ("suite has no active cases").

get.description:
List cases by suite (filtered, paginated) or by project (all suites).

Pass exactly one of ``suiteId`` or ``projectId``. The per-suite path applies
the full filter/keyset-pagination contract; the per-project path returns
every non-deleted case across the project's suites (used by the Cases tree
which groups cases under their suites). Both 404 when the target is
cross-workspace.

``?includeDeleted=true`` surfaces tombstoned rows. Per ``docs/API.md §3.3``
that capability is ADMIN/OWNER only — QA + VIEWER asking for it get 403.
Default behaviour (no query param / ``false``) hides every soft-deleted row
so VIEWER reads remain safe.

post.description: Create a test case + its steps + its tag set atomically.
Upsert a suite's cases + steps from a lifecycle payload (idempotent by sourceRef).

Apply ``action`` (delete / move / priority / tag-add / tag-remove) to ≤100 cases.

Single transaction (no partial apply). The Pydantic discriminator already
narrowed ``body`` to one variant; the service validates the 100-id cap,
the cross-workspace constraint, and the move-target before mutating. On
success returns ``{updated, auditIds}`` and emits one WS event per affected
case AFTER the commit so subscribers never observe a phantom event.

get.description:
Semantic (or lexical) test-case search within the workspace (M4-2).

Uses the configured local :class:`Embedder` (``SUITEST_EMBEDDINGS=fastembed``)
to rank by cosine similarity; falls back to lexical scoring when embeddings
are disabled so ZERO-tier search still returns results.

schema.title: Response Search Test Cases Api V1 Test Cases Search Get

delete.description:
Soft-delete a test case (set ``deleted_at=NOW()``).

Returns 204 on the active -> deleted transition. Returns 404 when the row
is cross-workspace, never existed, OR is already tombstoned — re-DELETE is
indistinguishable from "no such row" because the default LIST / GET
queries hide tombstones (per ``docs/API.md §3.3``).

get.description:
Return a case with its ordered steps (+ executable) and tags; 404 if cross-ws.

Tombstoned rows are 404 by default. ADMIN/OWNER can surface them via
``?includeDeleted=true`` (matches the LIST contract).

post.description: Clone a case + its steps + tags inside the same suite (new public id).

get.description:
Return test case steps as a runnable script (M2-12).

Walks ``step.code`` and wraps each step in the target framework scaffold.
Steps with no ``code`` render as TODO comments — always succeeds at ZERO
tier without LLM. Writes a ``code_exports`` row for audit traceability.

Supported ``target`` values: ``playwright`` (default), ``cypress``,
``selenium``. An unknown value returns 400 ``INVALID_EXPORT_TARGET``.
get.summary: Export test case as runnable code (docs/API.md §3.18)

post.description:
Restore a soft-deleted test case (clear ``deleted_at``).

Idempotent per ``docs/API.md §3.3``: re-POST after restore returns 204.
Returns 404 when the row is cross-workspace or never existed.

Ad-hoc shortcut: validate then delegate to M1c ``RunService.create_run``.

Pre-flight re-runs :func:`validate_steps` against the CURRENT workspace tier
+ strict-zero setting (a case authored under LOCAL/CLOUD that later flips to
ZERO+strict must not silently queue an unrunnable run). Validator failures
surface through the same canonical envelope as ``POST /test-cases`` so the
FE editor's error rendering doesn't fork. No ``runs`` row is created when
pre-flight fails — the validator raises BEFORE we enter ``RunService.create_run``.

On success returns 202 with ``runId`` / ``publicId`` / ``statusUrl`` /
``wsRoom`` so the FE can immediately deep-link to ``/runs/<publicId>`` and
subscribe to the live ``run:<id>`` channel.

get.description: Return a case's steps only (step editor pre-load); 404 when cross-workspace.

schema.title: Response Get Test Case Steps Api V1 Test Cases  Case Id  Steps Get

post.description: Append one step using a row-locked ``SELECT MAX(order)`` for race safety.

get.description: Full traceability grid for a project (docs/API.md §3.7); 404 if cross-ws.

post.description: Handle a GitHub inbound webhook.

schema.title: Response Receive Github Api V1 Webhooks Github Post

200.description: Event accepted — either ``ping`` (`{pong: true}`) or an event the receiver intentionally drops (``{ignored: true, reason: ...}``).

404.description: No matching project for the integration

post.description:
Handle a GitLab inbound webhook.

Flow:

1. Reject unsigned / wrong-token requests with 401 (constant-time compare
   lives in :func:`resolve_workspace_by_token`).
2. Parse the payload according to ``X-Gitlab-Event``; unknown events return
   200 ``unsupported_event`` without enqueuing.
3. Resolve the local project from the integration mapping; missing → 404.
4. SETNX dedup over ``(project_id, commit_sha, trigger=WEBHOOK_GITLAB)``;
   duplicate → 200 ``duplicate``.
5. Resolve the gating selection; empty → 200 ``no_gating_suite``.
6. Enqueue a Run via :class:`RunService.create_run` with
   ``triggered_by="webhook:gitlab"`` and ``user_id=None``; return 202.

schema.title: Response Receive Gitlab Api V1 Webhooks Gitlab Post

200.description: Event accepted but no run enqueued

404.description: No matching project for the integration

post.description: Handle an inbound Jira webhook (M1d-18).

schema.title: Response Receive Jira Api V1 Webhooks Jira Post

200.description: Event accepted but no update applied

get.description: List the workspaces the current user is a member of (active only).

schema.title: Response List Workspaces Api V1 Workspaces Get

post.description:
Create a workspace; the caller becomes its OWNER (bootstrap, ZERO-safe).

No membership/role gate — a user with zero workspaces must be able to make
their first one (dogfood blocker #1). Slug collisions return 409
``DUPLICATE_WORKSPACE_SLUG``.

Restore/clone a workspace from an export archive (M4-30).

Creates a NEW workspace owned by the caller from the archive's structural
test assets (projects/suites/cases/steps/requirements). Secrets are NOT in
the archive and must be re-entered manually. Schema-version mismatch → 400.

get.description: Return the workspace's autonomy level + overrides + effective map.

put.description: Set the workspace's autonomy level + overrides (ADMIN+, audited).

get.description: Return per-provider / per-kind spend + soft daily budget for the workspace.
List all per-user spend caps for the workspace. Admin or Owner only.

schema.title: Response List User Limits Api V1 Workspaces  Workspaceid  Cost User Limits Get

post.description:
Set (or replace) the per-user daily/monthly LLM spend cap.

Admin or Owner only. Setting ``dailyCapUsd`` or ``monthlyCapUsd`` to 0
means unlimited for that window.

delete.description: Remove a per-user spend cap. Admin or Owner only.
Clear the active config; tier downgrades to ZERO. 404 when none set.

get.description: Return the active LLM config (key redacted). 404 when none is set.

put.description: Set/rotate the provider + key, then recompute capabilities (M3-3).

get.description: List the curated model catalog for ``provider`` (query param).

post.description: Round-trip a 1-token completion against the (proposed) provider.

delete.description: OWNER-only slug-typed-confirm delete. Tombstones now, cleans up async.

get.description: Return one workspace; 403 when the caller is not a member.

patch.description: Patch General-tab fields. ADMIN+ required; ``slug`` is immutable.

post.description: Enqueue assembly of a portable workspace archive (M4-29). OWNER/ADMIN.

get.description: Poll an export job; surfaces the 24h presigned download URL when ready.
List all members of a workspace; 403 when the caller is not a member.

schema.title: Response List Workspace Members Api V1 Workspaces  Workspace Id  Members Get

post.description: Add a member by email + role. OWNER/ADMIN required; OWNER grant is OWNER-only.

delete.description: Remove a member. Self-removal is allowed except for the sole OWNER.

patch.description: Change one member's role. OWNER/ADMIN required; OWNER grant is OWNER-only.

LOGIN_BAD_CREDENTIALS.summary: Bad credentials or the user is inactive.

get.description: The response varies based on the authentication backend used.

detail.reason: Password should be at least 3 characters

get.description: Return the resolved capabilities, overlaying workspace config when present.
Lightweight liveness probe: ``{tier, status, uptimeSec}`` (docs/API.md §3.0).

schema.title: Response Get Capabilities Health Capabilities Health Get

get.description: Liveness probe — no DB / Redis touch.
